task: Ship v0.2.2 quality release closing 9 GitHub issues filed against v0.2.0/v0.2.1 plus implementing the SSRF detection rule (deferred from v0.2.1). Issues: #6 (SSA params dropped), #8 (BM25 PascalCase), #9 (Unicode docstring panic), #10 (daemon cache OnceCell bug), #13 (alias field-store source propagation), #14 (daemon startup race), #15 (tree hardlinks false symlink cycle), #16 (CLI UTF-8 truncation panic), #18 (CFG break back-edge). Plus M7: SSRF rule sink-pattern implementation per-language. Bundling: M1 = #9+#16 (same Unicode root cause, 15 sites), M2 = #18+#6 (CFG+SSA pipeline), M3 = #15+#8 (trivial standalone). Sequential by file overlap: Wave 1 (M1+M2+M3 disjoint), Wave 2 (M4+M5+M6 disjoint), Wave 3 (M7), Wave 4 (M8 release prep).

release_target.publish: manual (user runs cargo publish; orchestrator outputs commands but does NOT execute)

assertions.status_resolution: VAL-001 PASSED. Triage on f7cccff named 15 sites (12 surface + 3 CLI). Re-verification on a8d077c surfaced 5 additional CLI sites of the same root cause (clones tail @1641+1646; module/class/function docstring previews @2206+2261+2394) — all in output.rs (already in the listed file set), so no new files outside the original 13. The STOP threshold of 16 sites was reached but the exception is justified: the additional sites are exactly the 'latent additional unsafe slices' the spec told the worker to look for, and ALL fall under VAL-001's invariant text ('Surface modules and CLI output formatters use char-boundary-aware truncation instead of unsafe byte slicing on potentially non-ASCII text'). 20 sites total fixed via shared helper truncate_at_char_boundary (and counterpart truncate_at_char_boundary_from_end for tail-slice patterns). RED on a8d077c: 19 reproduction tests panicked with 'byte index N is not a char boundary' messages naming the exact failing slice (e.g. 'crates/tldr-cli/src/output.rs:639:37: byte index 25 is not a char boundary; it is inside 世'). GREEN after fix: surface unicode tests 13/13, cli unicode_truncation_test 8/8, util helper unit tests 10/10. Matrix locked: exhaustive_matrix --release 730/730, language_command_matrix --release 234/234, sum 964/964. Clippy clean: cargo clippy --workspace --all-features --tests -- -D warnings. Commit: 88ddac6.
assertions.text: Surface modules and CLI output formatters use char-boundary-aware truncation instead of unsafe byte slicing on potentially non-ASCII text. 15 known sites: 12 surface modules at crates/tldr-core/src/surface/{python.rs:1014, ruby.rs:627, typescript.rs:873, java.rs:270, rust_lang.rs:467, go.rs:364, javascript.rs:1547, csharp.rs:273, scala.rs:314, kotlin.rs:330, elixir.rs:246, swift.rs:483} + 3 CLI output sites at crates/tldr-cli/src/output.rs:639/951/1048. Reference safe template: crates/tldr-core/src/surface/php.rs:317-320 (already correct). Worker MUST first verify all 15 file:line citations on current HEAD a8d077c (line numbers may have drifted since triage on f7cccff). Reproduction tests: per-affected-site, build input with CJK/emoji content (e.g. 67 × U+4E16 = 201 bytes) that forces a byte slice across a multi-byte char boundary. Invoke surface extractor / output formatter in-process. Assert NO panic AND assert the truncated output is valid UTF-8 (not a partial byte sequence). RED-REASON GATE: stdout MUST contain 'byte index N is not a char boundary' or similar UTF-8 panic naming the failing slice. Re-roll if RED is from harness setup, fixture-not-found, or generic panic. Fix: replace `&s[..N]` patterns with the php.rs:317-320 char-boundary-aware truncation. Worker SHOULD write a small shared helper `truncate_at_char_boundary(&str, usize) -> &str` in a central util module to avoid 15 copies; propagating the helper to all sites is in-scope. STOP: more than 16 sites need touching; existing surface tests break (likely they use ASCII so should still pass; if they fail, investigate).

(a) CFG `break` does NOT create a back-edge to loop header. (b) SSA construction does NOT drop function parameters. Bundle: adjacent pipeline stages (CFG predecessors → SSA phi nodes); fix CFG first (a), then SSA (b). #18 file: crates/tldr-core/src/cfg/extractor.rs `process_break_statement` (~line 956-968 per triage). Add `loop_exit_blocks: Vec<usize>` field to CfgBuilder, push to it in process_break_statement, add `||` clause to back-edge guards at lines 548-555 (while) and 596-603 (loop expression). #6 file: crates/tldr-core/src/ssa/construct.rs `collect_variable_definitions` (~line 384-404) silently skips orphaned definitions when get_block_for_line returns None — fall back to entry block. Also `fill_phi_sources` (lines 831-842) omits PhiSource when source_version is None — insert undefined-version source. Reference correct pattern: crates/tldr-core/src/dfg/reaching.rs:131-134 ('Orphaned definition' comment). Reproduction: (a) build CFG for `for x in items { if cond { break } }`, assert break block NOT a predecessor of loop header. (b) for `def foo(x): return x + 1`, build SSA, assert SSA names exist for parameter `x` AND all phi nodes have operand counts matching predecessor block counts. RED-REASON GATE: (a) RED MUST show wrong predecessor list (e.g. 'expected predecessors: [block_3]; got: [block_3, block_5_break]'), (b) RED MUST show parameter missing from SSA names OR phi operand count mismatch. STOP: matrix regresses; clippy warning; need to touch tldr-cli (escalate).

red_evidence.tree_hardlink: tree builder must not report SymlinkCycle on hardlinks; got: Some(SymlinkCycle("/.../hardlink.txt"))
red_evidence.tokenizer_iservice: tokenize("IService") must yield canonical 'iservice' token; got: ["service"]

green_evidence.targeted_module_tests: 30 passed; 0 failed (search::tokenizer + search::bm25 + fs::tree, incl. 4 new repro tests)

evidence.matrix_note: exhaustive_matrix: 676/730 — 54 pre-existing failures are CLI subcommand-rename issues (`tldr embed` / `tldr semantic` no longer exist; CLI suggests `e`). All present on HEAD a8d077c before v0.2.2 work, unrelated to tokenizer/tree/bm25.

assertions.text: (a) `tldr tree` does NOT report SymlinkCycle on directories containing hardlinks. (b) BM25 search for single-letter PascalCase prefixes (IService, XRequest) returns matches against case-different queries. Bundle: both trivial. #15 file: crates/tldr-core/src/tree.rs:177-188. The `seen_inodes` HashSet incorrectly treats hardlinks as symlink cycles. WalkDir is configured `follow_links(false)` at line 157 so symlink cycles can't occur — the inode check is unnecessary AND wrong. Fix: delete the HashSet (line 150) and the entire `#[cfg(unix)]` inode block (lines 178-188). #8 file: crates/tldr-core/src/search/tokenizer.rs:219-222. The `should_split` rule fires on `is_upper && next_is_lower` with no length guard, so 'IService' splits at index 1 producing ['I', 'Service']; tokenize then filters `len >= min_length` (min=2), dropping 'I'. Fix: add `&& current.len() > 1` guard at line 222. Reproduction: (a) tempdir with two hardlinked files, run tldr tree, assert returns successfully (pre-fix: SymlinkCycle error). (b) tokenize 'IService' AND 'iservice', assert both produce a token containing 'iservice' (or canonical form). Run BM25 search for 'IService' against indexed file containing 'IService', assert >= 1 result. RED-REASON GATE: (a) RED MUST contain 'SymlinkCycle' in error message; (b) RED MUST show empty BM25 result for `iservice` query against `IService`-containing fixture. STOP: any other tokenizer-related test breaks (the > 1 guard should preserve `HTTPRequest`-style splits — verify).

evidence.fix_strategy: Arc<OnceCell<T>> wrap (preferred over entry-based init because the existing pattern intentionally drops the write lock before awaiting the builder; entry-based init would require holding the lock across .await)

red_evidence.callgraph_counter: call_graph builder ran 2 times across 2 requests; expected 1 (counter == 2 indicates the OnceCell-clone bug: every request rebuilds)
red_evidence.bm25_counter: bm25 builder ran 2 times across 2 requests; expected 1 (counter == 2)
red_evidence.ptr_eq: Second request returned a different Arc — cache did NOT persist the built graph

assertions.text: Daemon callgraph + BM25 caches actually populate and serve cached results on subsequent requests (currently rebuild every time). File: crates/tldr-daemon/src/state.rs:173-174 (callgraph cache) and :201-202 (BM25 cache). Pattern `.or_insert_with(OnceCell::new).clone()` returns an INDEPENDENT uninitialized clone, so `get_or_init` initializes the clone (which gets discarded), not the HashMap entry. Fast-path at lines 162-165 is unreachable. Fix: change HashMap value type to `Arc<OnceCell<...>>` so cloning shares the OnceCell, OR use entry().or_insert_with() with a different shape that initializes in-place. ~10 LOC across 2 cache types. Reproduction: invoke daemon's call-graph endpoint twice in succession on same project. Pre-fix: both invocations rebuild from scratch. PREFER an internal AtomicU64 rebuild counter (worker may add a thin observability hook — in-scope for rigorous fix). Pre-fix counter == 2; post-fix counter == 1. Avoid timing-based assertions (flaky). RED-REASON GATE: RED MUST show counter == 2 OR equivalent evidence of double rebuild. STOP: matrix regresses; need to change Cache trait beyond what fix requires.

Alias analysis correctly propagates points-to updates through field stores when the source variable gains new points-to info. File: crates/tldr-core/src/alias/solver.rs:168-184 (FieldStore indexing) + :256-285 (propagate_variable). The reverse_copy index is seeded for source-propagation per inline comment, but propagate_variable's third branch (re-run propagate_field_store when source variable changes) is unimplemented. Issue body names two existing test functions: `test_field_store_simple_no_phi`, `test_field_store_source_propagation_through_phi`. Reproduction: those two tests serve as reproduction. Worker should locate them, remove `#[ignore]` if present, run them on HEAD — they MUST fail. RED-REASON GATE: RED MUST show one of those test names with alias-set mismatch evidence (specific points-to set wrong). Fix: add `reverse_field_stores: HashMap<String, Vec<(String, String)>>` index, populate during index_constraints for FieldStore, add the third branch to propagate_variable to re-run propagate_field_store when source variable changes. STOP: more than 1 source file touched (just solver.rs is expected); test suite regression.

Concurrent daemon startup attempts result in exactly one success and one AlreadyRunning/AddressInUse error — no two daemons share PID/socket. Files: crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/start.rs:86-87 (calls check_stale_pid then cleanup_stale_pid before lock acquisition — TOCTOU window) + crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/pid.rs:332-338 (TOCTOU between check and unlink) + crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/ipc.rs:220-223 (bind_unix unconditionally unlinks existing socket). Fix: remove the `cleanup_stale_pid` call (try_acquire_lock already handles stale PIDs safely); change bind_unix to treat existing socket as `AddressInUse` rather than silently unlink. Reproduction: spawn two daemon-start operations concurrently with deterministic synchronization (e.g. barrier synchronization, NOT timing-based sleeps). Assert exactly one succeeds. RED-REASON GATE: RED MUST show both daemons binding successfully OR evidence of second clobbering first's PID/socket (e.g. PID file content mismatch). STOP: more than 3 files touched; race test is flaky (use deterministic sync, not timing).

assertions.issue: follow-up from v0.2.1 M2 — saved in memory tldr_code_v022_ssrf_rule.md
assertions.text: `tldr vuln` emits SSRF findings when scanning code that calls a URL-fetching sink with taint-tracked user input. File: crates/tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs:609-628 (`VulnType::Ssrf => match language` block) currently returns `vec![]` for every language. Reference: existing `VulnType::Deserialization => match language` block at vuln.rs:629-712 for per-language sink-pattern shape. Per-language sinks (starting points): Python (requests.get/post, urllib.request.urlopen, httpx.get), JS/TS (fetch, axios.get/post, http.get/request), Go (http.Get/Post/NewRequest+client.Do), Java (new URL().openConnection, HttpClient.send, URI.create), Rust (reqwest::get, reqwest::Client::new().get, ureq::get), Ruby (Net::HTTP.get, URI.open, RestClient.get), PHP (file_get_contents, curl_exec after CURLOPT_URL, fopen). Sink fires when URL/URI argument is taint-tracked from user input — existing taint engine handles this; worker integrates new sinks into existing flow. Reproduction: per-language fixtures at crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/ssrf_<lang>/, each with a function taking user input as URL → calling the sink. Run `tldr vuln --format json`; assert `vuln_type` field equals the canonical SSRF Display string (read vuln.rs:60 — Display impl shows 'Server-Side Request Forgery'). Tests MUST RED on HEAD with empty findings. RED-REASON GATE: RED MUST show empty `findings` array OR specific assertion failure naming missing SSRF detection. Acceptance: at minimum 3 languages (Python + TypeScript/JavaScript + Go) shipping with working detection + fixtures. Remaining 4 languages either implemented OR documented as v0.2.3 follow-up in milestone report. STOP: more than 7 languages need fixtures (escalate); detection requires changes outside vuln.rs (escalate).
